idea.title: Flip Dash
idea.hook: One tap flips gravity — sprint along the floor, flip to the ceiling, weave through every hazard. How far can you dash?
idea.hook_ru: Один тап переворачивает гравитацию — мчись по полу, переворачивайся на потолок, уворачивайся от всего. Как далеко ты умчишься?
idea.prototype: An auto-running avatar in a side-scrolling PORTRAIT corridor. ONE tap anywhere flips gravity so the avatar falls to / runs along the opposite surface (floor <-> ceiling). Dodge obstacles on both surfaces and floating in the middle; collect coins. Speed + obstacle density escalate with distance. Daily-seed run (same course for everyone today) + endless practice mode. Distance milestones unlock colour zones/biomes; coins unlock cosmetic avatar skins. Lose = collide with any hazard.
idea.mutation_of: Daily Dodge (daily_dodge) — our #1 game, dodge genre
idea.why_novel: fresh single-tap gravity-flip runner; first gravity-flip dodge in our portfolio; fixes Daily Dodge's 'mobile controls' complaint with one-tap control

sources.title: Daily Dodge — dodge genre, 100% like-ratio, ~72s/play
sources.snippet: Top game by engagement; extend the arcade-reflex/dodge family; one complaint was mobile controls

sources.title: Flappy Dash: Ultra! / Flappy Dude
sources.snippet: single-button reflex games trending on Yandex casual 2026-05-29 — external validation for one-tap loop

synthesizer_brief: KNOWLEDGE pick extending our #1 game Daily Dodge (dodge genre, 100% like-ratio) into a fresh single-tap gravity-flip runner. Built inside-out from our own engagement data, not a clone. Design thesis is the simplest possible mobile control (ONE tap anywhere = flip gravity), which directly fixes Daily Dodge's sole complaint ('mobile controls are not the best'). Honours the comment-theme hard requirements: crystal-clear goal in 5s, mobile-portrait-first with all controls thumb-reachable, and real depth (daily-seed + endless modes, distance milestone biomes, persistent coin economy, 6 unlockable skins, escalating obstacle patterns). Non-timer pressure = escalating speed + gravity. Externally validated by single-button reflex games (Flappy Dash: Ultra!, Flappy Dude) trending on Yandex casual.

alternatives.title: Lane Weaver
alternatives.angle: 3-lane swipe dodger where you swap lanes to thread incoming traffic; closer to Subway-Surfers but two-input — rejected today because it needs swipe (not one-tap) and overlaps the lane-runner space.
alternatives.why_not_built: two-input control conflicts with the one-tap thesis that fixes Daily Dodge's complaint

alternatives.title: Orbit Dodge
alternatives.angle: tap to reverse a ball's orbit direction around a central hub while debris flies in; one-tap reflex but radial rather than a corridor runner.
alternatives.why_not_built: radial framing reads less clearly as 'go far' and is harder to make crystal-clear in 5s than a horizontal runner

alternatives.title: Pulse Jumper
alternatives.angle: one-tap vertical jumper (Flappy-like) dodging gaps; closest to the trending single-button games but we'd be cloning Flappy directly rather than introducing the gravity-flip novelty.
alternatives.why_not_built: too close to existing Flappy clones; gravity-flip is the more novel, ownable mechanic for our portfolio
